original_abstract: In this study, a cerium-based metal–organic framework (Ce-MOF) was synthesized through a hydrothermal method using a 4,4′,4′′-nitrilotribenzoic acid linker. The resulting framework features a combination of Lewis acidic Ce³⁺ centers and triphenylamine cores, endowing the material with robust Fenton-like catalytic properties. Ce-MOF demonstrated exceptional efficiency in the oxidation of olefins, as well as primary and secondary alcohols. Specifically, the catalyst achieved a 100% conversion rate for cinnamyl alcohol and a 53% conversion for styrene, with high selectivity toward benzaldehyde (100%) and styrene oxide (75%), respectively. Beyond fine chemical synthesis, the Ce-MOF was utilized for the advanced oxidative degradation of organic water pollutants, including Rhodamine B (RhB), Methyl Blue (MeB), Congo Red (CR), and Direct Blue (DB), using hydrogen peroxide as an oxidant. Mechanistic investigations confirmed that the catalytic process relies on the generation of reactive hydroxyl radicals (•OH). Overall, this work highlights the potential of Ce-MOFs to enhance advanced oxidation processes (AOPs) for environmental remediation. Importantly, the catalyst exhibited excellent stability and recyclability, maintaining its performance over five consecutive cycles.
humanized_abstract: Recently, a cerium-based metal–organic framework (Ce-MOF) named as Ce-4,4′,4′′-N3TIB has been prepared using hydrothermal strategy employing 4,4′,4′′-nitrilotribenzoic acid as the organic linkers. The highly Lewis acidic sites of metal node Ce3+and the triphenylamine part of the organic linker rendered it as a highly stable Fenton-like heterogeneous catalyst. Its applications in heterogeneously catalytic oxidation of olefins, primary as well as secondary alcohols have also been studied. Remarkable yields of the targeted products have been obtained under optimized conditions using as prepared sample. The catalytic efficiency towards the oxidation of cinnamyl alcohol and styrene are 100% and 53%, respectively. The corresponding target products viz., benzaldehyde (100%) and styrene oxide (75%) are formed with very high selectivity and recovered catalyst exhibited good reusability up to 5-cycles. This as-prepared sample of Ce-MOF was also studied for the heterogeneous Advanced Oxidation Process (AOP) remediation of organic dyes such as Rhodamine B (RhB), Methyl Blue (MeB), Congo Red (CR), and Direct Blue (DB) using H2O2as an oxidant. •OH was considered as the major acting oxidizing species in heterogeneous catalytic reaction which thus supports the effectiveness of the obtained Ce-MOF as that of AOP catalysts.
